name: weight_perturbation
label: stage3_original
why: Add Gaussian noise to matrix weights after each optimizer step, scaled by LR. From Langevin dynamics: SGD + properly scaled noise samples from the posterior over weights, biasing toward flat (high-entropy) regions. Flat minima survive quantization better because nearby weight configurations have similar loss. Distinct from QAT (quantization-specific noise in forward), dropout (activation noise), SAM (adversarial perturbation).
validates: Noise-biased flat minima improve post-quant val_bpb. The loss landscape geometry matters for quantization.
falsifies: SGD stochasticity already provides enough implicit exploration. Explicit noise hurts convergence without improving flatness.
notes: Cross-domain: Langevin dynamics / statistical mechanics / channel coding.
Scale=0.01 means noise std = 0.01 * current_lr. Decays with the schedule.
If it wins, the mechanism is 'geometry of the minimum matters for quantization'.
If it loses with scale=0.01, try scale=0.001 before killing (noise too large).
